evidence.text: Freight revenues increased 29% during the second quarter of 2021 compared to 2020, resulting from a 22% volume increase, higher fuel surcharge revenue, and core pricing gains.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating due to significant revenue increases and volume growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: The increase in freight revenues was driven by higher fuel surcharge revenue and core pricing gains.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully raised prices, driving higher freight revenues.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Premium freight revenue increased in the second quarter and year-to-date periods compared to same periods in 2020 due to volume increases, higher fuel surcharge revenue, positive mix of traffic, and core pricing gains.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, improving profitability.

evidence.text: Freight revenues increased 29% during the second quarter of 2021 compared to 2020, resulting from a 22% volume increase, higher fuel surcharge revenue, and core pricing gains.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, supported by volume and pricing gains.

evidence.text: Revenue carloads increased 22% in the second quarter of 2021 compared to 2020, driven by higher demand across most commodity groups.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across most commodity groups, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: End markets such as intermodal, automotive, and industrial are growing fastest.

evidence.text: Intermodal shipments increased 21% in the second quarter of 2021 due to improving economic conditions, inventory restocking, contract wins, and continued strength of e-commerce and parcel shipments.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets like intermodal and automotive are growing rapidly, driven by economic recovery and e-commerce.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly fuel prices.

evidence.text: Operating expenses increased 17% driven by higher fuel prices, volume related costs, and higher casualty costs, partially offset by productivity.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs, particularly fuel prices, are rising, increasing operating expenses.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, but manageable.

evidence.text: Compensation and benefits increased 13% in the second quarter of 2021 compared to 2020, driven by wage inflation and hiring.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to wage inflation and hiring, posing a challenge.

evidence.text: Operating income increased 50% in the second quarter of 2021 compared to 2020, leading to an improved operating ratio of 55.1%.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are expanding due to a significant increase in operating income and improved operating ratio.

evidence.text: Productivity improvements partially offset higher fuel prices and volume related costs, contributing to the improved operating ratio.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements are offsetting higher costs, contributing to the improved operating ratio.

evidence.text: Supply chain disruptions continue, particularly in intermodal, due to high demand and constraints in ports, railroad equipment, and truck driver supply.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Supply chain disruptions continue to impact operations, particularly in intermodal.

evidence.text: Management is promoting vaccination incentives to mitigate COVID-19 spread and is investing in productivity improvements.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is taking proactive steps to mitigate risks and invest in improvements.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain disruptions and semiconductor shortages.

evidence.text: Supply chain disruptions continue, particularly in intermodal, due to high demand and constraints in ports, railroad equipment, and truck driver supply.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Three and Six Months Ended June 30, 2020

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include ongoing supply chain disruptions and semiconductor shortages.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Higher fuel prices and supply chain disruptions are impacting operating costs and revenue growth.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher fuel prices and supply chain disruptions are impacting operating costs and revenue growth.

factors.summary: Catalysts include inventory restocking and contract wins.
